goal: ChatPage displays real messages with real-time updates and users can send messages.
summary: Phase 2 complete. ChatPage rewired to use real messages via useMessages(conversationId), showing sender avatars, names, timestamps, and read status. Added Supabase Realtime subscription listening to postgres_changes INSERT on messages table filtered by conversation_id, invalidating queries on new messages for real-time updates. Send button wired to useSendMessage mutation with disabled state during pending. Messages marked as read on conversation view via useMarkAsRead. TypeScript compiles with 0 errors.
stepSummaries:
1. Refactor ChatPage to use useMessages(conversationId) and display real messages with sender info [verified]
  2. Add Supabase Realtime subscription in ChatPage: subscribe to postgres_changes INSERT on messages filtered by conversation_id, and invalidate messages query on new message [verified]
  3. Wire the chat input to useSendMessage with optimistic UI update (message appears immediately in the list) [verified]
  4. Mark messages as read when the conversation is viewed via useMarkAsRead [verified]
  5. Type-check after changes [verified]
